message:
fix(google-connectors): pin base_url host per connector + IPv6 [::1] + reject non-string overrides

Five connectors (google-admin-reports, google-calendar, google-docs,
google-drive, google-sheets) previously shared a loose
`host_is_googleapis` helper that accepted any `*.googleapis.com` host
(including the exact `googleapis.com` root). That matched the connector's
own API host plus any sibling Google API — a capability-escalation
surface, because a connector with a misconfigured base_url override
could transparently reach other Google services it has no business
contacting.

Tighten every validator to the single canonical host the connector
actually needs:

  google-admin-reports  →  admin.googleapis.com            (was any *.googleapis.com)
  google-calendar       →  www.googleapis.com              (was any *.googleapis.com)
  google-docs           →  docs.googleapis.com             (was any *.googleapis.com)
  google-drive          →  www.googleapis.com              (was any *.googleapis.com)
  google-sheets         →  sheets.googleapis.com           (was any *.googleapis.com)

Each connector's `host_is_googleapis` helper is renamed to
`host_is_<connector>_googleapis` and now does a single
`host.eq_ignore_ascii_case(CANONICAL_HOST)` comparison. The
`validate_*_base_url` function and its error-message format string pick
up the exact host name so operators see, e.g.,
"base_url must target docs.googleapis.com (localhost/127.0.0.1/::1
allowed for tests): evil.example.com".

Other hygiene inline with the pin:

- Every `is_local_test_host` helper now accepts `[::1]` in addition to
  `::1`, matching how `reqwest::Url::host_str()` renders
  link-local IPv6. Previously an `http://[::1]:9999/...` override was
  rejected because the bracketed form was never in the allowlist.
- Every connector's `configure` handler now treats an override of the
  wrong JSON type as an error rather than silently falling through to
  the default: `params.get("base_url")` is matched directly, and a
  non-string value (e.g. a JSON number or boolean) returns
  `FcpError::InvalidRequest { code: 1003, message: "`base_url` must be
  a string" }`. An explicitly-empty string still returns the existing
  "base_url must not be empty" error from the validator. Prior
  `.and_then(Value::as_str).map(str::trim).filter(|v| !v.is_empty())`
  chain silently treated non-string values as "no override" — now it
  fails fast with a clear diagnostic.
- google-calendar picks up the userinfo / query / fragment rejection
  branches the other four connectors already had, so all five validators
  agree on what a clean override looks like.

Adds per-connector regression coverage that
`configure_rejects_invalid_base_url_override` surfaces the right
`InvalidRequest { message contains "base_url" }` / "empty" error for
both the numeric-override and empty-string cases, plus
`host_is_*_googleapis_rejects_wrong_hosts_and_lookalikes` tests that
lock in the exact-host contract (so a future refactor that
accidentally re-widens the allowlist fails loudly).

The google-drive integration test
`connector_suite_happy_path_lists_files` picks up a rustfmt-driven
multi-line wrap on its `info!` macro call + the `println!(
"{}", serde_json::to_string(entry).expect("...") )` serialization line;
no test semantics change.

No production callers outside tests pass an override, so the tighter
policy does not break any running deployments — the default
`DEFAULT_BASE_URL` path keeps using the connector's canonical host
unchanged.
